scenario_cards: Ethanol bottle day: Inventory -> Add new bottle of Ethanol Absolute (from ethanol-absolute concept). Give it a lot number, expiration, assign location to lab-bench. Save. Verify it appears in the concept's 'physical bottles' list. Open the lab bench location -- verify the new bottle is a child. Screenshot every step.

Aliquot day: Take an existing sample (e.g. sample-pistachio-4). Create 6 new tube instances named tube-pistachio-4-aliquot-1 through -6, each pointing to the sample via of:. Pick an existing box OR create a new box box-pistachio-aliquots (grid 9x9) on shelf-minus80-a-1. Assign each tube to grid positions A1 through A6. Save. Open box popup, confirm all 6 aliquots render in the grid.

New freezer box day: Create new box 'Library Prep Kit Storage' (grid 10x10) on an existing shelf. Add 3 tubes into it at specific positions. Verify the box appears in the shelf children list, the tubes appear in the box grid, and each tube shows the right breadcrumb.
Reorder day: Scan inventory, pick 3 items that are actually low, toggle need-more on each. Verify dashboard needs-ordering count updates. Toggle one back off and verify it decrements.

Lab notebook full day: Create a new notebook entry under your own folder. Add a title, body with 3 section headers, a table of 4 columns x 6 rows, a callout, a wikilink to an existing protocol, upload 2 photos (plant growth, gel), annotate one photo with a label. Save. Reload. Verify all content renders correctly.

Print protocol day: Open a protocol with images. Click print. Verify the print preview opens, images load fully (not blank), pagination doesn't clip figures, headings stay with their sections. Screenshot the print preview.
Rename + relink day: Rename an existing protocol. Verify any wikilinks pointing to the old slug still work (redirects or auto-updated). Check backlinks in the connections panel.

Equipment move day: Pick 3 inventory items currently on shelf A. Use the locations picker to move all 3 to shelf B. Verify the tree view updates on lab-map page. Verify the old shelf no longer lists them as children.

New user onboarding: Create a new person card (frontmatter type=person, name, role, focus). Create a notebook folder for them. Create their first entry. Verify the person appears on the People page and their notebook sorts correctly.

Concept+instance linking: Pick a reagent concept with zero bottles. Create 2 physical bottle instances. Verify the concept's popup column 3 now lists both bottles. Verify inventory table hides the individual bottles under the concept row.

Cross-page wikilink crawl: From the dashboard, follow a link to a protocol. From the protocol, follow a wikilink to a reagent. From the reagent to a bottle. From the bottle to a location. Each step: screenshot + evaluate breadcrumb + verify page loads correctly.
Photo-heavy notebook: Upload 5 images to one notebook entry. Annotate 3 (boxes + labels). Resize 2 smaller. Save. Reload. Verify all 5 render at the right sizes, annotations visible, no broken paths.
Mobile lab notebook: On mobile viewport, create a notebook entry with a photo + text. Verify the Aa mobile toolbar works, the fab button layout, the image upload from mobile camera input.
Bulk sample tracker: Add 10 samples in rapid succession with varying status. Filter by status. Verify counts. Check a random sample's full detail page.
Search every surface: Global search for an obscure term (e.g. 'DNeasy' or a rare reagent name). Verify it returns hits across inventory, protocols, notebooks, wiki with correct context snippets.
Backlinks sanity: Pick any object with known inbound references (e.g. a sample referenced by tubes). Open its popup. Verify all backlinks listed, each one clickable, each destination loads.
Calendar add/delete: Add a new lab block at a specific hour. Verify it appears in the week grid. Delete it. Verify it disappears.
Waste container: Create a new waste container for ethanol-contaminated gloves. Verify days-held starts at 0 and updates on reload.

screenshot_protocol.rule_1: Every click, save, navigation, or form submit MUST have a before AND after screenshot.
screenshot_protocol.rule_2: Save all cycle screenshots under /tmp/qa-screenshots/cycleN/ (create the dir).
screenshot_protocol.rule_3: Name screenshots descriptively: cycleN-stepM-<persona>-<action>.png e.g. cycle9-03-vianney-aliquot-box-popup.png
screenshot_protocol.rule_4: After taking each screenshot, READ it with the Read tool. Do NOT skip. Evaluate: is the UI rendering correctly? Is the right object loaded? Are pills colored right? Are icons right? Is the breadcrumb correct? Is there overflow, clipping, or missing text?
screenshot_protocol.rule_5: Write a visual evaluation string per screenshot into the cycle's workflows_tested entry under screenshots: [{path, what_it_shows, evaluation, issues_found}].
screenshot_protocol.rule_6: If a screenshot reveals a visual issue (even a cosmetic one), log it as a bug with severity: visual. Attempt a fix if in scope (app/ or docs/ only).

bugs.summary: Dashboard Inventory Status card showed stale counts and alert list after inventory items had their status toggled on the inventory page
bugs.evidence: step09-after-dashboard-after-3-toggles.png is identical to step01 baseline despite 3 items being toggled to needs_more; programmatic check returned picksFound:[false,false,false] and alertCount unchanged
bugs.root_cause: dashboard.html init() called `fetch(BASE + 'object-index.json')` directly instead of going through Lab.gh.fetchObjectIndex, which applies the localStorage patch overlay populated by patchObjectIndex. The static object-index.json is only rebuilt on deploy, so between an inventory edit and the next deploy the dashboard rendered stale data.

fixes: dashboard.html init() now prefers Lab.gh.fetchObjectIndex (with its localStorage overlay) and falls back to raw fetch only if Lab.gh is unavailable (commit 763ad51)
observations: cycleStatus correctly walks the STATUS_CYCLE ring (in_stock → needs_more → out_of_stock → in_stock) and commits each step to GitHub.
Inventory page stat cards (In Stock / Needs More / Out of Stock) are immediately correct after each toggle — they read from the in-memory inventory array updated by cycleStatus.
Alert rows on the inventory table get a 4px amber/red left border and a light tint — consistent with the status pill color.
The Inventory Status card on the dashboard is fed from the same object-index, so once the overlay was applied the alert list auto-populated with no other changes.

Dashboard top-stat 'INVENTORY 480' is the raw total of *all* stock+resource objects (including externals), whereas the status counts (224/2/1) only sum tracked statuses — so they don't add up to 480. That is intentional but could confuse a lab member.
Verification file: tests/qa-cycle-3-verify.mjs toggles 2 items, navigates to dashboard, asserts picksFound:[true,true] — kept in the repo as a regression harness for the fix.

bugs.root_cause: patchObjectIndex in app/js/github-api.js whitelists fields against INDEX_KEYS. That list did not include the waste-container fields (contents, physical_state, container, hazard_class, started, waste_tag) or the sample-tracker fields (sample_id, species, lead, sequencing_type) even though the server-side scripts/build-object-index.py has them in EXTRACT_KEYS. Every save via patchObjectIndex therefore dropped these fields from the localStorage overlay, so pages that read them rendered blanks during the window between save and next deploy.

fixes: Added waste-container fields (contents, physical_state, container, hazard_class, started, waste_tag) and sample-tracker fields (sample_id, species, lead, sequencing_type) to INDEX_KEYS in app/js/github-api.js so the client list mirrors EXTRACT_KEYS in scripts/build-object-index.py (commit 5c01bed).

bugs.root_cause: fetchTree() in app/js/github-api.js read GitHub's git/trees recursive endpoint with no overlay layer. saveFile and deleteFile did not record their changes anywhere fetchTree could see, so any caller (notebooks.html sidebar, wiki.html, protocols.html) would render a stale tree until the API caught up.

bugs.summary: Notebook entries saved via notebooks.html did not appear in the link-index overlay — wikilinks pointing to sample-pistachio-4, qiagen-dneasy-extraction, etc. did not surface as backlinks on those targets until the next deploy

bugs.root_cause: The cycle-1 patchLinkIndex fix was wired into editor-modal.js's save flow but notebooks.html's saveDoc / createDoc / deleteDoc did not call any of patchObjectIndex / patchLinkIndex / removeFrom*, so notebook saves bypassed the overlay machinery that pages like editor-modal popups depend on.

fixes: fetchTree localStorage overlay (TREE_PATCH_KEY, 5-min TTL) — saveFile and deleteFile now record their path changes; fetchTree merges them into the API response so any caller (notebooks/wiki/protocols sidebars) sees the just-saved file immediately (app/js/github-api.js, commit e0a0f44)

notebooks.html saveDoc / createDoc / deleteDoc now apply patchObjectIndex + patchLinkIndex (and removeFrom* on delete) so the new entry shows up in the hierarchy + backlink panels of every wikilink target without waiting for a deploy (app/notebooks.html, commit e0a0f44)

bugs.summary: BREADCRUMB-DANGLING-SLASH — popup breadcrumb wrapped to two lines and left a standalone '/' separator at the end of line 1 with no following pill, looking like a dangling trailing slash. Visible on any popup with 4+ ancestor levels.
bugs.evidence: cycle1/step06-empty-9x9-box-popup.png, cycle7/08-empty-10x10-box-popup.png, cycle7-reshoot/01-box-popup-with-3-tubes.png — line 1 ends with 'Shelf 1 /' and the next pill ('Library Prep Kit Storage') wraps to line 2 alone
bugs.root_cause: In app/js/hierarchy.js breadcrumbHTML, each crumb pill and each '/' separator was rendered as an independent flex child of a flex-wrap container, so when the total width exceeded the container any flex item (including a bare separator) could be the last item on a wrapped line.

fixes: breadcrumbHTML now groups each separator with its following pill inside an inline-flex span so separator+pill wrap together as one atomic unit. The outer flex still wraps, but can only break between groups, never inside a group — so a '/' can no longer end up orphaned at a wrapped line boundary (app/js/hierarchy.js, commit 25c4999). Verified across 3 viewport widths (1440 / 900 / 520) via tests/qa-cycle-7-verify.mjs; endsWithOrphanSlash:false for all lines at all widths.
observations: 10x10 box grid renders 100 cells cleanly with A-J row labels and 1-10 column labels; occupied cells at all four boundary positions (A1 top-left, C5 middle, J10 bottom-right) render correctly — no edge-case rendering issues for corner cells.
The cycle-2 hierarchy cache invalidation is still holding — a new box shows up as a child of its parent shelf immediately on reopen, and the shelf's tree item-count increments without a redeploy.

Type system is consistent across 5-level chains: dark Room → light-blue Freezer → grey-blue Shelf → brown Box → teal Tube. Colors, icons, and pill shapes match across breadcrumb, fields column, parent pill, and contents list.

Top-nav NAV-TRUNCATION issue from earlier cycles is NOT reproducible at 1440px on the current deploy — all tab labels including 'Lab Map' fit fully. Only the far-right 'Apps' tab gets clipped (shows just 'A'). Either the menu was tightened since cycle 4 or the layout breakpoint changed.

Observed a minor NEW issue: the wikilink '[[dna-quantification-qubit]]' in the box popup body renders as a plain teal pill without a type icon, unlike sibling links to QIAGEN DNeasy Plant Extraction that get the purple book icon. Likely the target slug doesn't resolve in the object index — possibly a typo or an out-of-date link.
tests/qa-cycle-7.mjs, tests/qa-cycle-7-reshoot.mjs, and tests/qa-cycle-7-verify.mjs kept as harnesses: the verify script specifically asserts no orphan separators at viewport widths 1440 / 900 / 520.

bugs_found: BACKLINKS-LAG (functional, fixed in cycle 1): newly-created or freshly-edited files were missing from the backlinks panel of every wikilink target until the next deploy

HIERARCHY-CACHE-STALE (functional, fixed in cycle 2): after a save the Lab.hierarchy cache retained its pre-save snapshot, so parent CONTENTS + child breadcrumbs were missing for new objects until reload

STALE-DASHBOARD-INDEX (functional, fixed in cycle 3): dashboard Inventory Status card did raw fetch of object-index.json, bypassing the localStorage patch overlay — counts + alerts lagged until site redeploy

OF-FIELD-NOT-CLICKABLE (functional, fixed in cycle 4): the `of` field on bottle/tube popups rendered as plain slug text while the `parent` field was a clickable object pill — cross_linker could not navigate instance → concept from the fields column

WASTE-FIELDS-NOT-IN-INDEX-KEYS (functional, fixed in cycle 5): client INDEX_KEYS did not include the waste-container fields (contents, physical_state, container, hazard_class, started, waste_tag) or sample-tracker fields (sample_id, species, lead, sequencing_type) that the server-side EXTRACT_KEYS has, so patchObjectIndex dropped them — new waste containers showed blank Contents/State/Days Held until the next deploy and filters excluded them

FETCHTREE-NO-OVERLAY (functional, fixed in cycle 6): fetchTree returned GitHub's git/trees view directly with no localStorage overlay, so the notebooks/wiki/protocols sidebars showed pre-save folder counts after a reload until the API caught up (~30s)

NOTEBOOKS-SAVE-NO-PATCH (functional, fixed in cycle 6): notebooks.html saveDoc/createDoc/deleteDoc never called patchObjectIndex / patchLinkIndex / removeFrom*, so notebook saves bypassed the overlays — backlinks on wikilink targets did not include the new entry until the next deploy
TABLE-HEADER-HOVER-COLOR (cosmetic, open from cycle 6): a single table column header switches to teal text on hover/focus while siblings stay black, looking visually inconsistent
TOAST-LINGERS (cosmetic, fixed in cycle 8, commit be436a0): success toasts faded after 3s, errors after 6s, all toasts click-to-dismiss — Lab.showToast now picks duration by type

TOPNAV-LAST-ITEM-CLIPPED (cosmetic, fixed in cycle 8, commit ed22c7c): top-nav tab padding tightened from 12px 14px to 12px 10px (and 12px 7px below 1280px) so all 12 tabs fit at 1440-wide without clipping — nav.js
SEARCH-PLACEHOLDER-CLIPPED (cosmetic, fixed in cycle 8, commit dc5bf39): notebook sidebar search placeholder shortened from 'Search notebooks...' to 'Search...' — notebooks.html

STATUS-RAW-SLUG-IN-POPUP (cosmetic, closed): editor popup for a waste container showed Status='in_accumulation' as raw slug text; the editor-modal view-mode renderer now title-cases unknown status slugs and has explicit entries for waste statuses (CLOSED in 879fb98)
WIKI-404-FOR-UNSAVED-DEPLOY (ux, open): newly-created wiki docs 404 in the wiki viewer until the next deploy, even though the user is authenticated and Lab.gh.fetchFile could return the live file
NAV-TRUNCATION (cosmetic, fixed in cycle 8, commit ed22c7c): same fix as TOPNAV-LAST-ITEM-CLIPPED — tab padding tightened in nav.js so 'Lab Map' fits fully
MAP-PLACEHOLDER-OVERLAP (cosmetic, open): SVG placeholder for the floor-plan has overlapping text labels

BREADCRUMB-DANGLING-SLASH (cosmetic, fixed in cycle 7): popup breadcrumb wrapped to two lines and left a standalone '/' at line-end because each separator was an independent flex child — fix groups separator+pill inside inline-flex so the pair wraps together

DNA-QUANTIFICATION-PILL-UNTYPED (minor, open from cycle 7): wikilink to 'dna-quantification-qubit' in the libprep box body renders as a plain teal pill without a type icon — likely slug doesn't resolve in the object index or type inference failed

DASHBOARD-TOTAL-VS-STATUS-MISMATCH (cosmetic/ux, open): dashboard top card 'INVENTORY 480' counts all resource+stock objects while the Inventory Status counts (e.g. 226/0/1 = 227) only sum tracked statuses — the two numbers don't relate, which may confuse lab members

DUPLICATE-TITLE-IN-CONTENTS (cosmetic, closed): contents column of a location popup listed children with identical titles identically; renderChildrenListPane now detects sibling title collisions and appends a lightweight subtitle (lot+expiration, or slug tail) only on colliding rows (CLOSED in 377d51f)

Created a blank notebook entry with H1, two H2 sections, a 7-row NanoDrop results table, a callout, 4 wikilinks, a task list, and 2 small generated PNGs uploaded via Lab.editorModal.uploadImageBlob. Rushed double-saved. Reloaded; all rich elements re-rendered correctly including image data URLs via the GitHub-API live fallback. Created a new 10x10 box 'Library Prep Kit Storage' on shelf-minus80-a-1 plus 3 tubes at A1 / C5 / J10 to stress grid rendering at top-left, middle, and bottom-right corners. Verified the shelf now lists the box as a child, the empty grid renders 100 cells, the filled grid correctly places the 3 tubes with teal fill + label fragments, and each tube's 5-level breadcrumb renders with all correct type-colored pills. Fix groups separator+following-pill inside an inline-flex span so wrap can only break between groups (commit 25c4999). Verified post-deploy at 3 viewport widths (1440/900/520) via tests/qa-cycle-7-verify.mjs — endsWithOrphanSlash:false for every line. All cycle-7 test artifacts cleaned up via gh API. Also noted a minor new issue (DNA-QUANTIFICATION-PILL-UNTYPED) for follow-up. NAV-TRUNCATION is no longer reproducible at 1440 — the 'Lab Map' tab now fits fully; only a far-right tab gets clipped.
